title: Quality Manager (Up to $5500 / Tuas)
description:
Quality Manager
$4500-$5500
Mon-Fri 8am-5.30pm, Sat 8am-12pm (Alt)
Sun & PH off
Location at Tuas


-Bonus
-After confirmation increment
-Yearly increment
-14 days annual leave (Max 18 days)
-Medical benefits


Authorities and Responsibilities

  1. Responsible for providing directives for the Quality Assurance Department in meeting management objectives.
  2. Formulate, implement and evaluate quality strategies programs for the company.
  3. Implement and maintain a system of auditing the facility and periodic calibration of test, measuring equipment and tools.
  4. Manage customer complaints, corrective and preventive action programs.
  5. Administer and manage continuous improvement program for all QA processes.
  6. Work closely with auditors, customers, production and suppliers to meet the quality requirements.
  7. Manage sub-contractors for on-time supply of treatment process, as well as meeting technical requirement.
  8. Review / prepare, administer and implement Quality Assurance Plans, Inspection Test Plans and other Operational procedures in accordance to the technical specifications, customer's surveillance requirements, and good manufacturing / engineering practices, and enforce the same on Operations in their execution.
  9. Work closely among inter-department to ensure committed schedules are met in a timely and effectively manner.
  10. Review and approve disposition program / action for nonconforming products.
  11. Ensure that processes needed for the quality management system are established and implemented.
  12. Report to top management on the performance of the quality management system, and note needed improvements.
  13. Ensure the promotion of awareness of statutory, regulatory and customer requirements throughout the organization.
  14. Ensure and coordinate the implementation of risk assessment and risk control of medical devices

The Quality Assurance Manager plays a crucial role in business by ensuring that products meet certain standards of quality. He/She plans, directs or coordinates quality assurance programs and formulate quality control policies. He/She also works to improve an organisation's efficiency and profitability by reducing waste. He/She prepares and update quality documentation, basing their processes on a recognised standard such as ISO 9000 – Quality Management, published by the International Organisation for Standardisation. He/She supervises teams of inspectors who carry out the detailed assessment of products and their components at different stages of production. He/She recruits and trains supervisors, and provides them with documented quality standards as guidelines for their daily work. He/She also selects quality inspection tools and software to support the inspection team.


Competence Requirements:

  • Diploma in Mechanical Engineering / Quality Engineering or equivalent.
  • Minimum 6 years of solid experience in Quality Management in similar or related industry and 3 years in management level.

~ Assist in the implementation of company quality systems.
~ Conduct Quality Assurance audit.
~ Manage cause investigation for non-conformance.
~ Manage company quality systems.
~ Manage departmental activities and deployment.
~ Able to commute between Tuas Plant and Loyang Plant.
